title: Biodegradable Polymer from Algal Biomass
disclosure_text: A method for producing biodegradable polymers from algal biomass using a novel enzymatic extraction process. The process operates at room temperature and pressure, reducing energy costs by 80% compared to traditional polymer synthesis. The resulting polymer degrades completely in marine environments within 6 months, addressing the microplastics crisis. Key innovation: a two-stage enzymatic cascade that first breaks down algal cell walls, then polymerizes the released monomers into a thermoplastic with tunable mechanical properties.
